summary: Typosquat of codebuff CLI fetches and executes opaque binary from unrelated GitHub account
details: Package `codebuff-cli` is a name-extension typosquat of the legitimate `codebuff` CLI published by CodebuffAI. The README is copied verbatim from the legitimate package (including the install instruction `npm install -g codebuff` and links to codebuff.com / the CodebuffAI GitHub org), so installers reasonably believe they are running the real Codebuff tool. On first CLI invocation, `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` downloads `codebuff-<platform>-<arch>` from `https://github.com/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/download/v<version>/...`, writes it to `~/.codebuff/bin/codebuff`, chmods it 0755, and execs it with the user's argv. The GitHub account `Marcus-Mok-GH` is unrelated to the legitimate publisher (CodebuffAI); no hash or signature verification is performed on the fetched bytes; no source for the binary is shipped in the package. The combination of (a) impersonation of a real product via a copied README, (b) a binary fetch from a publisher-mismatched account, and (c) absence of any integrity verification means any installer who runs this CLI executes arbitrary bytes controlled by an unrelated third party.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
